program.name: Modern Manners & Mental Fortitude

founder.name: Michael R. Terry
founder.title: Founder, MMMF · Utah Highway Patrol Sergeant · MAT Candidate

program.tagline: The life skills course your students actually need.
program.mission: Transfer, not coverage. Students leave able to apply concepts independently to unfamiliar, high-stakes situations.
program.copyright: © Michael R. Terry. This material is protected intellectual property.

implementation_tracks.name: Grades 7–8
implementation_tracks.label: Foundational
implementation_tracks.description: 1 term per year. Introductory complexity. Peer, classroom, and family contexts.
key_notes: Emphasis on formative assessment and reflection journals

implementation_tracks.name: Grades 9–12
implementation_tracks.label: Leveling Up Annually
implementation_tracks.description: 1 term per year. Complexity increases each grade. Spiral design ensures no content repeated at same level.

grade_progression.grade_11: Dissimilar transfer. Workplace and digital dilemmas.
grade_progression.grade_12: Full capstone, novel scenario, authentic audience.

implementation_tracks.name: Community Youth
implementation_tracks.label: Outside School · Youth Program
implementation_tracks.description: Flexible delivery. Cohort-based or workshop format. Community centers, faith orgs, after-school.
key_notes: Flexible pacing to fit program schedule

implementation_tracks.name: Community Adult
implementation_tracks.label: Outside School · Adult Program
implementation_tracks.description: Workplace, family, and personal development contexts. Same frameworks, adult-relevant scenarios.
key_notes: Pre/post CASEL-adapted surveys for program evaluation
Co-workers, partners, authority figures as scenario contexts

core_pillars.title: Accountability & Repair
topics: Own it, fix it, follow through

PLRR.name: Regulation Routine
PLRR.purpose: Self-regulation under stress — used BEFORE decision-making

steps.name: PAUSE
steps.description: Stop the automatic reaction. Create space before responding.

steps.name: LABEL
steps.description: Name the emotion. What am I actually feeling right now?

steps.name: REFRAME
steps.description: Shift perspective. What is actually happening vs. what I assumed?

steps.name: RESPOND
steps.description: Choose deliberately — aligned with values and context.

POCC.name: Decision-Making Framework
POCC.purpose: Structured choice-making under pressure and competing priorities

steps.name: PAUSE
steps.description: Regulate first. Good decisions require cognitive space.

steps.name: OPTIONS
steps.description: Identify multiple possible responses. What are my real choices?

steps.name: CONSEQUENCES
steps.description: Evaluate short and long-term outcomes. Who is affected and how?

steps.name: CHOOSE
steps.description: Select a response and justify it. Be able to explain the reasoning.

POCC.note: Always teach PLRR before POCC. Students cannot make good decisions when dysregulated.

learning_targets.description: Recognize and actively use respectful communication approaches across school, community, workplace, and digital contexts.
Use a decision-making framework (POCC) to analyze scenarios and justify choices with evidence and likely consequences.
Apply self-regulation strategies (PLRR) during conflict or stress, using the routine as a performance skill, not a therapeutic one.
Navigate digital and in-person etiquette with attention to permanence, reputation, and audience — in school, community, and online settings.
Explain how accountability — owning a mistake, repairing it, and following through — builds trust and long-term opportunity.
Establish personal growth targets, monitor progress through self-assessment, and independently transfer all skills to an unfamiliar, high-stakes scenario.

four_week_cycle.description: Core concepts introduced through mini-lessons, case studies, graphic organizers, and instructor modeling.
Students apply learned skills in structured practice. Scenario prompts build fluency before independent application.
Team-based tasks reinforce understanding. Reflection journals connect experience to concept.

spiral_design.principle: All four pillars revisited every cycle at increasing levels of complexity.

16_week_pacing.topic: Respectful Communication: Active listening, sentence stems
Guest Speaker: First Responder on Composure ★
Modern Etiquette: Digital, public, school settings

formative.name: Weekly Reflection Journals
formative.description: Five guided prompts each week tracking metacognitive growth.
prompts: What was one skill or idea you practiced this week?
How did it go when you applied it outside the classroom?
What would you do differently next time?
How did someone else demonstrate good manners or emotional control this week?
What is one goal you want to set for next week?

formative.name: Concept Sorting & Exit Reflections
formative.description: Determines whether students can accurately define and distinguish anchoring concepts.

formative.name: Scenario Analysis & Role-Play Rubrics
formative.description: Observes whether students apply regulation and decision frameworks during structured practice.

formative.name: Pre/Post Self-Efficacy Surveys
formative.description: CASEL-based competency surveys at course start and end.

summative.name: How I Handle It Capstone
summative.description: Students present a real or simulated scenario walking through course strategies.

rubric.exceeds: Clear, relatable, thoughtfully chosen with real-world grounding
Demonstrates mastery with nuanced, layered application
Insightful, honest, shows genuine growth awareness

summative.name: Novel Scenario Performance Task
summative.description: Unfamiliar, high-pressure scenario mixing school, digital, and community contexts. Real-world authentic audience.

theoretical_foundations.author: Tyler
theoretical_foundations.application: All goals intentional, measurable, and aligned with student needs. Backward design from capstone to daily objectives.

theoretical_foundations.author: Bruner
theoretical_foundations.application: Core concepts revisited with increasing complexity across all five modules.

theoretical_foundations.author: Dewey
theoretical_foundations.application: Real-world role-play and simulations ground learning. Reflection journals connect experience to concept.

theoretical_foundations.author: Skinner
theoretical_foundations.application: Peer feedback, instructor recognition, and Manners in Motion challenges reinforce positive skill application.

theoretical_foundations.author: Wiles & Bondi
theoretical_foundations.application: CMP links context, goals, implementation, and evaluation into a coherent whole.

theoretical_foundations.author: CASEL
theoretical_foundations.application: Self-management, interpersonal communication, responsible decision-making, digital citizenship aligned to Utah CCA Core Standards.

theoretical_foundations.author: Stern et al.
theoretical_foundations.application: Anchoring concepts, compelling questions, and dissimilar transfer tasks drive unit structure.

theoretical_foundations.author: Lang
theoretical_foundations.application: Brief, frequent retrieval and reflection cycles build durable habits.

compelling_questions: How do my everyday choices shape how others trust me — and the opportunities I get?
What does respect look like when no one is watching?
Why do strong emotions make good decisions harder — and what can I do about it in the moment?
How should my behavior change depending on context: friend group, classroom, workplace, or online?
